floor_history.headline: fable-blitz deep recon: csmd4 IS marionation region-1's cheaper twin (no region-3) - the whole gap is the in-place-basin v1/a0 allocation-equation flip (pri(arg5) > pri(t0)); the wrap-weighting x in-p

Structural axis (decl order, s32/u32 narrow, statement re-assoc under arg5-after-t0) closed directly on csmd4: 4/4 masked-INERT at 2, head-order flip regresses to 7 - twin's structural closure confirm

Structural axis on csmd4 h5 base fully closed: 13 variants swept (var-splits, expression compression, decl-position, pointer-deref form) — 8 INERT at masked 2, 5 measured regressions (t0s-split=3, arg
Frontier top-3 cleared: do-while(0) wrap direction-invariant +2 insns (KILLS pri-lift lever), cnt-carrier regresses to masked 4, volatile fmt-carrier policy-rejected. h5 floor holds at 2.
s5 permuter: all 3 frontier hypotheses (block-scope multi-set carrier, named-dispatch reorder, nested do-while(0)) KILLED with measurements; h5 masked-2 floor holds

frontier.hypothesis: The g3 base (masked 6, ORDER PERFECT, residual = pure v1/a0 exchange) may admit a duplicated-statement-into-arms lever: duplicating the arg5 index computation into distinct control-flow arms (SOTN-sanctioned 2026-07-01, duplicated-statement-into-arms.md) creates a byte-neutral REG_N_REFS priority lift on arg5-qty without triggering cross-jump merge collapse.
frontier.mechanism: flow.c REG_N_REFS lift from duplicated statement (2x refs) achieves the +differential the wraps failed to deliver, but WITHOUT the loop-note ref-multiplier's symmetric effect on t0. Ownership-sanctioned since 2026-07-01 with byte-neutrality + FAKE-annotation prerequisites.
frontier.next_probe: Apply g3 form (in-call addu, t0<<=2 statement) to src/system.c; guard the arg5 stage under an if/else duplication (both arms compute the same arg5 index) - each arm's ref-set counts once, cross-jump may re-merge byte-neutrally, giving arg5-qty refs=4 without loop-notes. Measure masked and window-diff vs g3's 6.

frontier.hypothesis: A hybrid form combining h5's v0-staging (arg5 chain) with the g3-style t0<<=2 STATEMENT variant (not the in-call addu) may create a new qty configuration untested by either pure base.
frontier.mechanism: Prior sweeps only tested each base independently: h5 uses `t0 = (s32)((u8*)tbl + t0)` in the outer scope THEN in-call *(s32*)t0; g3 uses `t0<<=2` in-block + in-call *(s32*)(t0 + tbl). The hybrid — h5's v0 chain PLUS g3's t0<<=2 form — represents 8 unmeasured cells in the h/g cross-product ledger's Cartesian.
frontier.next_probe: Sweep 4 cells: {h5-v0-staging, g3-v0-inline} x {h5-t0-in-call-t0, g3-t0<<=2-plus-in-call-addu}; measure each via sandbox --disable all; look for a cell with masked <2 or masked =2 with a distinct register configuration.

frontier.hypothesis: Since ALL prior wrap probes symmetrically lift both idx sources, an asymmetric wrap that includes idx_1494[1] but NOT idx_1494[0] should selectively lift arg5-qty refs while leaving t0-qty untouched.
frontier.mechanism: flow.c REG_N_REFS depth multiplier applies per-insn based on which loop the insn resides in; if idx_1494[0] is emitted BEFORE the wrap begins, only idx_1494[1] gets the ref lift.
frontier.next_probe: src/system.c: `t0 = idx_1494[0]; do { v0 = idx_1494[1]; v0 <<= 2; arg5 = *(s32*)(v0 + (s32)tbl); } while(0);` — only the arg5 chain inside the wrap, with t0 assignment BEFORE the wrap. (This differs from s4 P1 which wrapped {v0=idx[1]; v0<<=2; arg5=...} but scored masked=20 +2 insns — the discovery here is the +2 barrier signature that F3/F3b/F4 lack, indicating placement-dependence in the loop-note barrier effect.) Re-measure and diff-compare with F3's no-barrier signature.
